# Mincha Gedola

[Back to all zmanim](index.md)

## Mincha Gedola 16 Point 1 Degrees

Mincha gedola according to the Magen Avraham, with the day beginning and ending when the sun is 16.1° below the horizon. It is described as the earliest time to pray mincha.

Calculated as 6.5 solar hours after alos, using a day that begins at alos 16.1°. If the setting to use astronomical chatzos for other zmanim is enabled, it is calculated as half of a half-day shaah zmanis between chatzos hayom and alos 16.1°, after chatzos hayom.

This time may not be available in northern or southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle or north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not reach low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMinchaGedola16Point1Degrees`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMinchaGedola16Point1Degrees`

## Mincha Gedola 30 Minutes

Mincha gedola calculated as 30 regular clock minutes after chatzos. Some use this time to delay the start of mincha in the winter when half of a shaah zmanis is less than 30 minutes.

Calculated as a fixed 30 minutes after chatzos, rather than half of a shaah zmanis after chatzos.

One should not use this time to start mincha before the standard GRA mincha gedola.

See Shulchan Aruch [Orach Chayim 234:1](https://hebrewbooks.org/pdfpager.aspx?req=49624&st=&pgnum=291) and the Shaar Hatziyon seif katan ches.

Because this is a fixed 30 minutes after chatzos, it is affected by the setting for astronomical chatzos, not by the setting for using astronomical chatzos for other zmanim.

This time may not be available in places such as the Arctic Circle, where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMinchaGedola30Minutes`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMinchaGedola30Minutes`

## Mincha Gedola 72 Minutes

Mincha gedola according to the Magen Avraham, with the day beginning 72 minutes before sunrise and ending 72 minutes after sunset. It is described as the earliest time to pray mincha.

Calculated as 6.5 solar hours after alos, using a day that begins 72 minutes before sunrise. If the setting to use astronomical chatzos for other zmanim is enabled, it is calculated as half of a half-day shaah zmanis between chatzos hayom and tzais 72 minutes, after chatzos hayom.

This time may not be available in places such as the Arctic Circle, where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMinchaGedola72Minutes`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMinchaGedola72Minutes`

## Mincha Gedola Ahavat Shalom

Mincha gedola based on the opinion of [Rabbi Yaakov Moshe Hillel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yaakov_Moshe_Hillel), as published in the luach of the Bais Horaah of Yeshivat Chevrat Ahavat Shalom. Mincha gedola is described as the earliest time to pray mincha.

Calculated as half a shaah zmanis after chatzos, with shaos zmaniyos based on a day starting at alos 16.1° and ending at tzais 3.7°, 13.5 minutes after sunset. The time used is the later of this calculation or 30 regular clock minutes after chatzos.

The setting to use astronomical chatzos for other zmanim does not apply here, because this zman involves chatzos offset from the center of the astronomical day.

This time may not be available in northern or southern locations, even south of the Arctic Circle or north of the Antarctic Circle, where the sun may not reach low enough below the horizon for this calculation.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMinchaGedolaAhavatShalom`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMinchaGedolaAhavatShalom`

## Mincha Gedola Ateret Torah

Mincha gedola based on the calculation of Chacham Yosef Harari-Raful of Yeshivat Ateret Torah. It is described as the preferred earliest time to pray mincha according to the opinion of the [Rambam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maimonides) and others.

Calculated as 6.5 solar hours after alos, where the day starts 1/10th of the day before sunrise and usually ends 40 minutes after sunset.

The end of the day is usually 40 minutes after sunset, but that offset is configurable to any offset.

This time may not be available in places such as the Arctic Circle, where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMinchaGedolaAteretTorah`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMinchaGedolaAteretTorah`

## Mincha Gedola Baal Hatanya

Mincha gedola according to the Baal Hatanya. Mincha gedola is described as the earliest time one can pray mincha. The [Rambam](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maimonides) is of the opinion that it is better to delay mincha until mincha ketana, while the [Ra"sh](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Asher_ben_Jehiel), [Tur](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jacob_ben_Asher), [GRA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vilna_Gaon), and others are of the opinion that mincha can be prayed lechatchila starting at mincha gedola.

Calculated as 6.5 sea-level solar hours after netz amiti, sunrise, based on the Baal Hatanya’s opinion that the day is calculated from sunrise to sunset.

This time may not be available in places such as the Arctic Circle, where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMinchaGedolaBaalHatanya`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMinchaGedolaBaalHatanya`

## Mincha Gedola GRAFixed Local Chatzos 30 Minutes

[Rav Moshe Feinstein’s](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moshe_Feinstein) calculation of mincha gedola, the earliest time one can pray mincha according to the [GRA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vilna_Gaon).

Calculated as 30 minutes after fixed local chatzos.

This time may not be available in places such as the Arctic Circle, where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMinchaGedolaGRAFixedLocalChatzos30Minutes`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMinchaGedolaGRAFixedLocalChatzos30Minutes`

## Mincha Gedola GRAGreater Than 30

The later of GRA mincha gedola and mincha gedola calculated as 30 minutes after chatzos. In the winter, when half of a GRA shaah zmanis is less than 30 minutes, the 30-minutes-after-chatzos time is used; otherwise, the GRA mincha gedola time is used.

Calculated by comparing GRA mincha gedola with 30 minutes after chatzos and using the later time.

Because this calculation can be an offset from chatzos when 30 clock minutes is later than half of a shaah zmanis, it may be affected by the setting for astronomical chatzos.

This time may not be available in places such as the Arctic Circle, where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMinchaGedolaGRAGreaterThan30`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ComprehensiveZmanimCalendar.getMinchaGedolaGRAGreaterThan30`

## Mincha Gedola GRA

The latest mincha gedola according to the [GRA](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Vilna_Gaon). Mincha gedola is the earliest time one can pray mincha. The Ramba"m holds that it is better to delay mincha until mincha ketana GRA, while the Ra"sh, Tur, GRA, and others hold that mincha can be prayed lechatchila starting at mincha gedola.

Calculated as 6.5 shaos zmaniyos, or solar hours, after sunrise. The day is measured from sunrise to sunset, using either sea-level sunrise and sea-level sunset or elevation-adjusted sunrise and sunset, depending on the elevation setting.

This time may not be available when the calculation cannot be computed, such as in the Arctic Circle where there is at least one day a year when the sun does not rise and one when it does not set.

??? info "Technical details"
    Source method: `ZmanimCalendar.getMinchaGedolaGRA`

    Technical reference: `com.kosherjava.zmanim.ZmanimCalendar.getMinchaGedolaGRA`
